title: Protein Aggregation Capture and Automated Processing for Proteomics
author: ronan o'cualain
materials: After the program is complete, remove any residual liquid carefully, and add 50 µL trypsin at a 1:20 to 1:100 ratio in 50 millimolar (mM) ammonium bicarbonate. Cover the plate with a sealing film, and place on a thermomixer overnight at 37 °C .

steps.title: Briefly vortex the beads and place...
steps.instruction: Briefly vortex the beads and place the tube on a magnetic stand for 00:02:00 to collect the beads.

steps.title: Add ultrapure water at a volume...
steps.instruction: Add ultrapure water at a volume corresponding to 5 to 10 times the initial volume of mixed beads.Vortex the beads for 00:00:10 and place on a magnetic stand for 00:02:00 to collect the beads. Carefully aspirate and discard the wash buffer with a gel loading tip.

steps.title: Repeat the wash steps a further two times
steps.instruction: Repeat the wash steps a further two times.

steps.title: Resuspend the beads with ultrapure water...
steps.instruction: Resuspend the beads with ultrapure water at a final concentration of 20 µg/µL .

steps.title: Washed beads may be stored at...
steps.instruction: Washed beads may be stored at 4 °C for up to one month.

steps.title: To the Eppendorf 96 well /...
steps.instruction: To the Eppendorf 96 well / 500uL plate which contains the samples in HEPES pH 7.5 to be processed, add 5 µL of the washed beads as prepared above. Washed beads (prepared as above) may be added to the samples in a ratio of 5-10 µg of beads to 1 µg of protein. Check the pH of any sample, it is critical that it is in the range of Ph7.0 pH 7.0 to Ph pH 8.5 for optimal binding by measuring an aliquot on pH paper.

steps.title: Under the Method tab, select home...
steps.instruction: Under the Method tab, select home all axes to orient and prepare the automated liquid handler.

steps.title: Under File , select Open/Method
steps.instruction: Under File , select Open/Method. Select the PACmethodV01 method.

steps.title: To the deck of the NxP,...
steps.instruction: To the deck of the NxP, add four p1000 tip boxes. SEE BELOW Add 1 Beckman full reservoir containing 150 mL of 100 % volume acetonitrile and 1 Beckman full reservoir containing 150 mL of 70 % volume acetonitrile. deck layout

steps.title: To each of the samples in...
steps.instruction: To each of the samples in the Eppendorf 96 well 500uL plate with the Resyn beads, add 300 µL of 100 % volume acetonitrile. MIx briefly on a plate shaker, then place on the Biomek instrument.

steps.title: Start the method by clicking the green Run icon
steps.instruction: Start the method by clicking the green Run icon. Run method

steps.title: You will be prompted by the...
steps.instruction: You will be prompted by the software to enter the location of the first column to be processed. If your first samples are in column A1 to H1 on your sample plate, enter " 1 " You will then be prompted to enter the value of the last column to be processed. If you have a half plate of 96 samples, enter "6". The method will need to be modified if more samples on the plate are to be processed. This is easily done, but more pause steps will need to be built in to accommodate the extra pipette boxes needed.

steps.title: The software will ask you to...
steps.instruction: The software will ask you to check that the deck layout matches that of the program. Once you are satisfied that this is the case, click OK . Running the PAC method.

steps.title: The workstation will pause after the...
steps.instruction: The workstation will pause after the acetonitrile washing steps, prompting the user to replenish used tips, and refill reservoirs if needed. Pause Do not reach into the workstation while the program is running, this action will break the "light curtain" and stop the system as a safety precaution.

steps.title: After digestion, remove the supernatant, leaving the beads behind
steps.instruction: After digestion, remove the supernatant, leaving the beads behind. This may be done by modifying the transfer file method, or manually by using a gel loading tip.

steps.title: Add 100 µL of 0
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL of 0.1 % volume formic acid to the beads, place the plate on a plate shaker for 00:02:00 , remove supernatant and pool eluates.
